closes #1303 

## To-do
<!---  list of proposed tasks in the PR, move to "Content" on completion 
- Proposed task
-->
- PR in climaArtifacts (https://github.com/CliMA/ClimaArtifacts/pull/130)
- high res data still needs to be moved into the correct directory in `/groups/esm/ClimaArtifacts/artifacts/soil_params_gupta2020_2022/`. Currently this directory still contains the old low res, depth-averaged data. 

## Content
<!---  specific tasks that are currently complete 
- Solution implemented
-->
- Add support for low/high res soil hydrology data paths in `Artifacts.jl`. Since previous global use cases all use the low res version, `lowres = true` by default. Typical use case for high res might be fluxnet/site level runs
- added a test to check if new soil maps are indeed depth varying (not depth averaged values)

title: Add support for high/low res vG param maps, fix depth varying bug

body:
closes #1376 

<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
If this PR is too hard to review because of the movement of functions from photosynthesis.jl to photosynthesis_farquhar.jl and the movement of functions out of canopy_parameterizations.jl, I can move things back and do that in a separate PR

## Purpose 
- Regular Farquhar works entirely in leaf level fluxes, and therefore (1) `GPP` needs to be upscaled to canopy level from A, and (2) stomatal conductance `gs` needs to be upscaled to canopy level with LAI. The Vcmax, Jmax, Rd, etc are all leaf level. However, the bug is that we pass in APAR at the canopy level, not leaf level. so we were blending leaf+canopy in computing A, and sort of upscaling GPP and gs twice.
- Pmodel: works entirely in canopy level: uses APAR_canopy, predicts GPP = A. Therefore, conductance computed from A is already canopy level. Jmax,Vcmax, Rd are all canopy level. The bug is that we pass in the correct APAR, but upscale GPP and conductance when we dont need to.
- Downstream autotrophic respiration and SIF use quantities computed by the photosynthesis models and we need to be consistent with which values are leaf or canopy level. The bug is that right now they get different quantities from the pmodel and farquhar model.
- Diagnostics also need to be consistent between the two. the bug is that we return Vcmax25 canopy in one case, and leaf level in the other case, same for Rd, etc.
- I moved farquhar specific functions and model/param definitions to photosynthesis_farquhar.jl, and moved pmodel specific functions to pmodel.jl, and likewise for autotrophic_respiration.jl. In a future PR I want to move more out of canopy_parameterizations.jl - that file is too long and hard to read.

## To-do
Revisit autotrophic model and SIF model to make sure we are passing the correct things. This PR should be behavior preserving in the sense of passing e.g. Vcmax25 leaf still, An_leaf, Rd_leaf...but Im not sure if that is what we should be passing

## Content
Diagnostics: GPP (canopy gross photosynthesis), vcmax25_leaf, rd_leaf, An_leaf now consistent between pmodel and farquhar
Bug fix: GPP is gross, not net: we should not subtract Rd from it. This is consistent with pmodel papers and Bonan's book
Bug fix: Farquhar works entirely with leaf level, we now pass APAR_canopy/LAI.
Bug fix: Pmodel works entirely with canopy level: no need to upscale to get GPP from A or when computing conductance of the canopy
Helper functions to get Vcmax25, Jmax, etc of _leaves_ from the pmodel - and rename variables to make it clear what is canopy vs leaf in downstream SIF and autotrophic respiration


<!---
Review checklist

I have:
- followed the codebase contribution guide: https://clima.github.io/ClimateMachine.jl/latest/Contributing/

- checked that this PR does not duplicate an open PR.

In the Content, I have included

title: Photosynthesis cleanup and bug fixes

body:
<!--- THESE LINES ARE COMMENTED -->
## Purpose 
Previously, we used Unions of component model types to dispatch on. This PR makes the small cleanup change of instead using abstract types for dispatch.

closes #1327
title: dispatch on abstract canopy types
